subject: Mathematics (NCERT - Ganita Prakash)
chapter: Connecting the Dots (Geometry - Lines and Angles)

activities_projects.title: Point and Line Hunt
activities_projects.objective: Identify basic geometric elements in surroundings
steps: Look around your room and find three examples of a 'Point' (e.g., a nail head)
Find three examples of a 'Line Segment' (e.g., edge of a table)
Sketch these in your notebook and label them clearly
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes points, lines, and segments in the real world

activities_projects.title: Angle Maker with Sticks
activities_projects.objective: Understand how angles are formed by two rays
steps: Join two sticks at one end using clay to form a vertex
Move the sticks to create a wide opening and a narrow opening
Draw the resulting shapes and label the 'Vertex' and 'Arms'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the structure of an angle
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct labeling of arms and vertex (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Complementary Corner
activities_projects.objective: Identify angles that add up to 90 degrees
steps: Draw a right angle ($90^{\circ}$)
Draw a line inside it to split it into two angles (e.g., $30^{\circ}$ and $60^{\circ}$)
Verify their sum and label them as 'Complementary Angles'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Grasps the concept of complementary angles

activities_projects.title: Supplementary Straight Line
activities_projects.objective: Identify angles that add up to 180 degrees
steps: Draw a straight line and mark a point on it
Draw a ray starting from that point to create two adjacent angles
Measure both angles and check if their sum is $180^{\circ}$
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that a linear pair is supplementary

activities_projects.title: Clock Angle Observations
activities_projects.objective: Identify angles in a circular clock face
materials: Wall clock or drawing of a clock
steps: Observe the clock at 3:00 (Right angle)
Observe the clock at 2:00 (Acute angle)
Observe the clock at 4:00 (Obtuse angle)
Sketch these three positions and name the angles
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Categorizes angles based on their magnitude
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct classification of angle types (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'X' Marks the Spot
steps: Cross the two strips and pin them in the center so they can rotate
Measure the two angles facing each other
Rotate the strips and measure again to see if they stay equal
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that vertically opposite angles are always equal
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate recording of angle measurements (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Letter Hunt: F, Z, and X
steps: Find the letters 'F', 'Z', and 'X' in a large headline
Highlight the angles formed within these letters
Identify which letter shows 'Alternate' (Z) and 'Corresponding' (F) positions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes transversals and parallel line angles in typography
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct matching of letters to angle types (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Parallel Rail Tracks
activities_projects.objective: Understand the property of parallel lines
steps: Draw two horizontal lines using the opposite edges of a ruler
Measure the distance between them at three different points
Note down if the distance remains the same
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Defines parallel lines through constant distance

activities_projects.title: The Transversal Path
activities_projects.objective: Identify a line intersecting two other lines
steps: Draw two parallel lines
Draw a third line that crosses both (the Transversal)
Color the interior region (between the parallel lines) differently from the exterior
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the transversal and the regions it creates
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear visual distinction of regions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Angle Sum of a Triangle (Paper Fold)
activities_projects.objective: Verify the total angles in a triangle
steps: Cut out any triangle and label the corners 1, 2, and 3
Tear off the corners and arrange them side-by-side on a straight line
Observe if they form a perfect semi-circle ($180^{\circ}$)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visually proves the Angle Sum Property
activities_projects.evaluation: Successful alignment on a straight line (10 marks)
